3.3. GNSS PWV retr ieval algorithm
Zenith wet delay (ZWD) and global weighted tempera-
ture (Tm) are the prerequisites for estimating PWV. In
our article, ZHDs calculated from ERA5 data are sub-
tracted from ZTDs estimated from PPP or PPP-B2b solu-
tions to obtain ZWD and the Tm can be calculated from
the Global Pressure and Te

H.Geetal. AdvancesinSpaceResearch77(2026)297–309
Fig. 8 shows that the summer ZTD rises to 2.4–2.8 m
with larger fluctuations. The two ZTD series are generally
synchronized, while there are some minor deviations,
which correspond to the RMSE of 10.0 mm in summer
(Table 2). This is mainly driven by intense changes in water
vapor. The results for the accuracy of PPP-B2b ZTD are
consistent with existing studies (Yang et al., 2023; Wang
et al., 2024), which also noted the seasonal variability of
ZTDs.
